action.reasoning: Observation: Iteration 7 training failed with CUDA OOM using batch_size=256 on the 512-hidden, 4-layer model that now includes temporal attention. Analysis: The attention block reshapes to [B×N, T, C], which roughly doubles activation memory. Halving batch_size to 128 should bring peak memory comfortably under the 31 GB limit while preserving the architectural upgrades (temporal attention, stronger horizon weighting, cosine LR). Decision: Rewrite train.py with batch_size=128, fix the truncated syntax, keep all other hyperparameters, suppress per-epoch loss prints (write to metrics.jsonl only), and relaunch training for 60 epochs. Immediately submit the resulting checkpoint for evaluation.

command_output.stdout: Training complete. Best val loss 0.4005 at epoch 1
